title: Migration Regulations 1994 - selected cancellation and response provisions

sections.heading: Grounds for cancellation under section 116
sections.text: The Migration Regulations 1994 include regulation 2.43, which sits in the cancellation generally subdivision and concerns grounds for cancellation connected with section 116 of the Migration Act. This regulation should be checked where the Department relies on the general power to cancel a visa.

sections.heading: Invitation to comment and timing provisions
sections.text: The Regulations also include regulation 2.44 on invitation to comment and regulation 2.46 on the time to respond to a notice of cancellation under section 129(1)(c). These provisions are directly relevant when reviewing whether a visa holder was given a procedurally proper chance to respond and whether deadlines were complied with.

sections.heading: Revocation and related cancellation machinery
sections.section_ref: Migration Regulations 1994 reg 2.48 and related provisions
sections.text: The cancellation subdivision also includes regulation 2.48 on revocation of cancellation and related provisions dealing with personal cancellation powers and other cancellation machinery. When a cancellation matter is escalated, these provisions help frame what documents and timelines need to be checked against the notice issued to the client.
